assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Verdict table lines 99-105 lists all 5 criteria individually as PASS: (1) single license filter via validate_license_param and is_in(), (2) comma-separated filter via split/Condition::any()/is_in(), (3) 400 validation via spdx::Expression::parse() rejection, (4) pagination integration with filter applied before count() and offset/limit, (5) unchanged PaginatedResults<PackageSummary> return type. Each criterion-N.md file confirms PASS with code-level reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 9: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 PR files match the task specification exactly (0 out-of-scope, 0 unimplemented) |'. Detailed findings (lines 33-38) list: Task 'Files to Modify': list.rs, service/mod.rs; Task 'Files to Create': tests/api/package.rs; PR files match exactly; Out-of-scope files: none; Unimplemented files: none.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR |'. Line 8: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate |'. Lines 165-167: 'No reviews or comments exist on this PR. Steps 4b-4c (convention loading and classification) were skipped per the skill specification.' Lines 169-170: 'No sub-tasks were created in Step 6d (no review feedback, no CI failures).'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are verification/report artifacts: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report describes reading and analyzing the PR diff (e.g., 'Scanned all added lines (lines with + prefix) in the PR diff', 'The PR diff modifies...') but contains no indication of code file creation or modification. The skill produced only read-only analysis outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements the license filter feature as specified in TC-9101.' There is no merge action, no call to merge APIs, and no indication of auto-merging. The report is structured as a findings presentation (verdict table + detailed findings sections) for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines |'. Detailed findings (lines 66-78): 'Scanned all added lines (lines with + prefix) in the PR diff. Checked for hardcoded passwords/secrets, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials. No matches found.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 16: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created; no modified or deleted test files |'. Detailed findings (lines 153-160): 'tests/api/package.rs: new file (+80 lines), 4 test functions, 0 removed. No modified or deleted test files -- sub-agent spawn for structural/semantic analysis is not needed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md organizes verdicts under four domain headings: (1) '### Intent Alignment' (line 27) contains Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A); (2) '### Security' (line 64) contains Sensitive Pattern Scan (PASS); (3) '### Correctness' (line 82) contains CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); (4) '### Style/Conventions' (line 119) contains Test Quality verdicts (Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS) and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). All expected verdicts from all four domains are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 33-38): file-by-file comparison listing task 'Files to Modify' and 'Files to Create' vs PR files, with out-of-scope and unimplemented counts. Security (lines 70-77): describes scanning 'all added lines (lines with + prefix)', lists checked pattern categories (passwords, API keys, private keys, env files, cloud credentials, database credentials), reports 'No matches found' with description of actual diff content. Correctness (lines 93-107): per-criterion table with code-level summaries, plus 5 separate criterion-N.md files each containing code snippets, test verification, and analysis. Style/Conventions (lines 127-160): per-test function analysis for repetitive test detection with algorithm-vs-data reasoning, doc comment excerpts for all 4 tests, and per-file classification evidence.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation appear as top-level sections separate from the four domain sections. Lines 165-167: '### Review Feedback -- N/A' states 'No reviews or comments exist on this PR. Steps 4b-4c (convention loading and classification) were skipped per the skill specification.' Lines 169-170: '### Root-Cause Investigation -- N/A' states 'No sub-tasks were created in Step 6d (no review feedback, no CI failures). Root-cause investigation is not applicable.' Both are presented independently from the domain analysis sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) and provide their own standalone rationale.
